messages.content: What are the best museums in Paris?
What are the best museums in Paris?

Louvre — iconic museum with the Mona Lisa and sprawling collections from antiquity to modern art; use the Paris Museum Pass or prebook to skip long lines. Musée d'Orsay — world-class Impressionist and Post‑Impressionist masterpieces housed in a beautiful converted railway station. Centre Pompidou — the buzzy hub for modern and contemporary art with striking architecture and great city views from the upper levels.
Which of those are good for kids?
What are the best museums in Paris?
What are the best museums in Paris?

Louvre — iconic museum with the Mona Lisa and sprawling collections from antiquity to modern art; use the Paris Museum Pass or prebook to skip long lines. Musée d'Orsay — world-class Impressionist and Post‑Impressionist masterpieces housed in a beautiful converted railway station. Centre Pompidou — the buzzy hub for modern and contemporary art with striking architecture and great city views from the upper levels.
Which of those are good for kids?
In Paris, the Cité des enfants, Luxembourg Gardens, and the Trocadéro/Palais de Chaillot (with its nearby aquarium) are the most kid‑friendly picks from that list.

attractions_found: Louvre — iconic museum with the Mona Lisa and sprawling collections from antiquity to modern art; use the Paris Museum Pass or prebook to skip long lines.
Musée d'Orsay — world-class Impressionist and Post‑Impressionist masterpieces housed in a beautiful converted railway station.

steps.prompt: Which of those are good for kids?
steps.response: Routing to Attractions: User asked which museums are good for kids, so call Attractions to list family-friendly features, activities and practical tips.

steps.prompt: Which of those are good for kids?
steps.response: In Paris, the Cité des enfants, Luxembourg Gardens, and the Trocadéro/Palais de Chaillot (with its nearby aquarium) are the most kid‑friendly picks from that list.
attractions_found: Cité des enfants (Cité des Sciences et de l'Industrie) — a hands‑on, age‑tailored science museum built for kids with interactive exhibits and workshops.
Luxembourg Gardens & Palais du Luxembourg — spacious gardens with playgrounds, puppet theatre, model sailing pond and plenty of room to run.
Place du Trocadéro / Palais de Chaillot (and nearby Aquarium) — wide open viewpoints of the Eiffel Tower plus museums and an aquarium that captivate children.
